log_points_added.snippet: console.log(`[traversal-debug] attachEdgeSensor: created zone ${edge} sceneKey=${scene?.scene?.key ?? 'UNKNOWN'} x=${x} y=${y} width=${w} height=${h}`)

console.log(`[traversal-debug] overlap fired: edge=${edge} sceneKey=${scene?.scene?.key ?? 'UNKNOWN'} playerX=${_px} playerY=${_py}`) — placed BEFORE the existing try/callback so it fires even on short-circuit

console.log(`[traversal-debug] performSeamlessTransition: scene=${fromKey} edge=${edge} targetSceneId=${adj?.to ?? 'NONE'} vx=${_peekVx} vy=${_peekVy} facing=${_peekFacing} adjacencyLookup=${JSON.stringify(adj ?? null)}`) — surfaces adjacency lookup result so missing entries are visible
console.log(`[traversal-debug] scene.scene.start() about to be called: target=${adj.to} data=${_startDataStr}`) — _startDataStr wrapped in try/catch around JSON.stringify(startData)

console.log(`[traversal-debug] applySeamlessEntry: scene=${scene?.scene?.key ?? 'UNKNOWN'} data=${_dataStr}`) — _dataStr wrapped in try/catch around JSON.stringify(data); placed at the very top before the !data || !data.entryEdge guard so even no-op invocations are visible

The startData object literal contents are byte-identical to the original. No control flow, no early returns, no positional math, no zone bounds, no adjacency lookup logic touched.

section_8_declaration.act_1_to_3_scaling_strategy: traversal primitive is act-agnostic; instrumentation applies uniformly across all scene-pair adjacencies

user_runtime_protocol: 1. npm run dev, 2. Open game and load save with Zuzu in NeighborhoodScene, 3. Walk east off the canvas edge, 4. Open DevTools console, 5. Filter by '[traversal-debug]', 6. Note WHICH log lines fire vs which are silent — silent links reveal where the chain breaks. Common patterns: (a) only attachEdgeSensor logs fire, no overlap → sensor zones positioned wrong or player has no physics body, (b) overlap fires but performSeamlessTransition early-returns → adjacency lookup miss (typo or missing data), (c) performSeamlessTransition fires but no scene.scene.start log → callback wiring broken, (d) scene.scene.start fires but no applySeamlessEntry on dest → init flow broken on DryWashScene side, (e) applySeamlessEntry fires but player ends up wrong → coordinate calculation bug. Paste log output back to dispatch Phase 2.
notes: Phase 1 instrumentation only. Phase 2 fix gated on user reading log sequence first. Static-only verdict. Speculation (NOT applied to code): static read of seamlessTraversal.js suggests the likeliest culprits, in order: (1) attachEdgeSensor at line 351 reads world size at call-time via getSceneWorldSize(scene); if NeighborhoodScene's create() runs attachEdgeSensor BEFORE the world bounds / scene.getWorldSize() is configured, the east zone could be positioned at scale.width=800 default rather than the actual world edge — sensor would sit far inside the visible canvas or far outside it. (2) The overlap zones use scene.add.rectangle which depth-defaults to 0; if the player sprite's body is set up with a moveTo / world bounds clamp that prevents it from reaching x=world.width-4, the overlap simply never collides. (3) SCENE_ADJACENCY.NeighborhoodScene.east.to is 'DryWashScene' — confirm scene key registration in main.js / Phaser config matches that exact string. The Phase 1 logs disambiguate all three: (1) shows up as zones logged with stale x values, (2) shows up as zone-creation logs but no overlap-fired log, (3) shows up as performSeamlessTransition with targetSceneId=DryWashScene followed by scene.scene.start firing but DryWashScene never logs applySeamlessEntry.
